revelation.en: a surprising fact that is made known, especially one that had been secret

ex.en: The report contained shocking revelations about the company.
ex.ko: 그 보고서에는 그 회사에 대한 충격적인 폭로 내용이 담겨 있었다.

revenge.en: action taken to hurt someone in return for a wrong they did to you

ex.en: She wanted revenge for what he had done to her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 자신에게 한 짓에 대해 복수하고 싶었다.

revenue.en: the total amount of money that a business or organization earns from its activities

ex.en: The company's revenue increased by 20% last year.

revenue.en: money that a government collects, especially through taxes

ex.en: Tax revenue funds public schools and roads.

reverence.en: a feeling of deep respect or awe for someone or something

ex.en: He spoke of his teacher with great reverence.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 스승에 대해 깊은 존경심을 담아 말했다.

reverse.en: change something to its opposite or make it go the opposite way

ex.en: The court reversed its earlier decision.

reverse.en: the opposite of what is usual or expected

ex.en: To everyone's surprise, the reverse happened.
ex.ko: 모두의 예상과 달리 그 반대의 일이 일어났다.

reverse.en: moving backward, or arranged in the opposite order

ex.en: He put the car in reverse gear.

revival.en: a return of interest, activity, or popularity after a period of decline

ex.en: There has been a revival of interest in vinyl records.
ex.ko: 바이닐 레코드에 대한 관심이 다시 살아났다.

revival.en: a series of meetings held to reawaken religious faith

ex.en: The church held a revival last summer.
Doctors managed to revive the patient.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그 환자를 소생시킬 수 있었다.

revive.en: bring something back to activity, popularity, or health after a period of weakness

ex.en: The government hopes to revive the economy.

revolution.en: a major, often violent, change in a country's government or political system

ex.en: The revolution overthrew the king.

revolution.en: a dramatic and far-reaching change in the way people think or do things

ex.en: The internet caused a revolution in communication.

revolutionary.en: involving or causing a complete change in the way things are done

ex.en: This is a revolutionary new technology.

revolutionary.en: a person who takes part in or supports a political revolution

ex.en: He was a famous revolutionary during the war.
Smartphones have revolutionized the way we communicate.
ex.ko: 스마트폰은 우리가 소통하는 방식에 대변혁을 일으켰다.

revolve.en: turn around a fixed central point

ex.en: The Earth revolves around the sun.

revolve.en: have something as the main focus or subject

ex.en: Her whole life revolves around her children.
ex.ko: 그녀의 삶 전체는 아이들을 중심으로 돌아간다.

rhinoceros.en: a large, heavy animal with one or two horns on its nose

ex.en: The rhinoceros is an endangered species.

rhyme.en: (of words) have the same last sound

ex.en: "Cat" rhymes with "hat".

rhyme.en: a similarity of sound between the endings of words

ex.en: The poem is full of clever rhymes.

rhythmic.en: having a regular pattern of sound or movement

ex.en: The dancers moved to the rhythmic beat of the drums.

rib.en: one of the curved bones that form the chest

ex.en: He broke a rib in the accident.

rib.en: a cut of meat that includes a rib bone

ex.en: We ordered a plate of barbecue ribs.
ex.ko: 우리는 바비큐 갈비 한 접시를 주문했다.

rider.en: a person who rides a horse, bicycle, motorcycle, or similar vehicle

ex.en: The rider guided the horse over the fence.
ex.ko: 그 기수는 말을 몰아 울타리를 넘게 했다.

ridge.cue: 산 사이로 길게 뻗은 높은 곳
ridge.en: a long, narrow area of high land along the top of hills or mountains

ex.en: They hiked along the mountain ridge.

righteousness.en: the quality of being morally right or good

ex.en: He lived a life of righteousness and honesty.
She was rightly praised for her hard work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열심히 일한 것에 대해 당연히 칭찬받았다.

rim.en: the outer edge of something round, such as a cup, wheel, or glasses

ex.en: She wiped the rim of the glass.
He ripped the letter open.
There's a rip in your jeans.

risky.en: involving the possibility of danger, failure, or loss

ex.en: Investing all your money in one stock is risky.
ex.ko: 돈을 전부 한 주식에 투자하는 것은 위험하다.

rite.en: a formal ceremony or action, especially a religious one

ex.en: The couple performed a traditional wedding rite.
ex.ko: 그 부부는 전통 결혼 의식을 치렀다.

ritual.en: a set of actions performed regularly in a particular order, often as part of a custom or ceremony

ex.en: Drinking coffee every morning is part of his ritual.
ex.ko: 매일 아침 커피를 마시는 것은 그의 일상 의식이다.

rival.en: a person or group competing with another for the same goal

ex.en: The two companies are fierce rivals.

rival.en: be as good as or equal to something else

ex.en: Few restaurants can rival this one for quality.
ex.ko: 이 식당의 품질에 필적할 만한 곳은 거의 없다.

roam.en: move around an area without a fixed purpose or direction

ex.en: They roamed the streets all afternoon.
The lion roared loudly in the night.
We heard the roar of the engine.

rocket.en: a vehicle or device that is propelled into the air or space by burning fuel

ex.en: The rocket launched into space successfully.
House prices rocketed last year.

rocky.en: covered with or full of rocks

ex.en: The path was steep and rocky.
Their marriage went through a rocky period.
ex.ko: 그들의 결혼 생활은 힘든 시기를 겪었다.

roommate.en: a person you share a room or home with

ex.en: My roommate and I split the rent.
ex.ko: 내 룸메이트와 나는 월세를 나눠 낸다.

rosy.en: having a pink color, especially from good health

ex.en: The baby had rosy cheeks.

rosy.en: showing signs of being good or successful

ex.en: The company's future looks rosy.

route.en: a way or path taken to get from one place to another

ex.en: We took a different route to avoid traffic.
ex.ko: 우리는 교통 체증을 피하려고 다른 경로로 갔다.

ex.en: The royalty attended the ceremony.

royalty.en: money paid to the owner of a patent, copyright, or resource for its use

ex.en: The author receives a royalty for every book sold.
ex.ko: 그 작가는 책이 팔릴 때마다 인세를 받는다.

rub.en: move something back and forth against a surface while pressing on it

ex.en: She rubbed her eyes because she was tired.

rubble.en: broken pieces of stone or brick from a destroyed building

ex.en: Rescuers searched through the rubble for survivors.

rug.en: a piece of thick fabric used to cover part of a floor

ex.en: There is a colorful rug in the living room.

ruinous.en: causing serious harm, damage, or financial loss

ex.en: The company suffered ruinous losses last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 막대한 손실을 입었다.

rural.en: relating to the countryside rather than the city

ex.en: She grew up in a rural area.

rush hour.en: the time of day when traffic is heaviest because people are traveling to or from work

ex.en: The roads are always busy during rush hour.
The old bicycle was completely rusty.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 자전거는 완전히 녹슬어 있었다.

rusty.en: not as good as before because of lack of practice

ex.en: My Spanish is a bit rusty since I haven't used it in years.
ex.ko: 몇 년 동안 안 써서 내 스페인어 실력이 좀 녹슬었다.

sack.en: a large bag made of paper, plastic, or cloth used for carrying things

ex.en: He carried a sack of potatoes.
He was sacked after missing too many deadlines.
ex.ko: 그는 마감을 너무 많이 놓쳐서 해고당했다.

sag.en: sink, bend, or hang down, especially from weight or weakness

ex.en: The old sofa sagged in the middle.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 소파는 가운데가 축 처졌다.

sag.en: a place or shape that sags

ex.en: There's a noticeable sag in the roof.
ex.ko: 지붕에 눈에 띄게 처진 부분이 있다.

sage.en: a very wise person, especially an old one

ex.en: The village sage gave them advice.

sage.en: a herb with grey-green leaves used for flavoring food

ex.en: She added sage to the stuffing.

sail.en: travel on water in a boat or ship

ex.en: They sailed across the Atlantic Ocean.

sail.en: a large piece of fabric that catches the wind to move a boat

ex.en: The sail filled with wind and the boat sped up.
ex.ko: 돛에 바람이 가득 차자 배가 속도를 냈다.

salary.en: a fixed amount of money paid to an employee, usually monthly

ex.en: She earns a good salary as an engineer.

salesmanship.en: skill in persuading people to buy things

ex.en: His salesmanship helped the store break records.
ex.ko: 그의 판매 수완 덕분에 그 가게는 기록을 경신했다.

salesperson.en: a person whose job is to sell goods or services

ex.en: The salesperson helped me choose a jacket.
ex.ko: 그 판매원은 내가 재킷을 고르는 것을 도와주었다.

saleswoman.en: a woman whose job is to sell goods or services

ex.en: The saleswoman recommended a better deal.
ex.ko: 그 여성 판매원은 더 좋은 조건을 추천해주었다.

salon.en: a shop where people go to have their hair or beauty treatments done

ex.en: She got her hair cut at the salon.

salsa.en: a spicy sauce made from tomatoes, onions, and chili peppers

ex.en: We dipped chips in salsa.
ex.ko: 우리는 칩을 살사 소스에 찍어 먹었다.

salsa.en: a lively Latin American dance style

ex.en: They took salsa lessons together.
The soup was too salty for me.

sandstone.en: a type of rock formed from grains of sand

ex.en: The canyon walls are made of red sandstone.
ex.ko: 그 협곡의 벽은 붉은 사암으로 이루어져 있다.

sanitary.en: clean and free from germs, or relating to hygiene

ex.en: The kitchen must be kept sanitary at all times.

sanitation.en: the systems for keeping places clean and healthy, especially by removing waste

ex.en: The city improved its sanitation system.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 위생 설비 시스템을 개선했다.

satisfactory.en: good enough for a particular purpose; acceptable

ex.en: Her performance was satisfactory but not outstanding.

saving.en: money that you keep instead of spending, or the act of not spending money

ex.en: She puts some of her salary into savings every month.

saving.en: an amount of money or time that is not used or wasted

ex.en: Buying in bulk gives a saving of about 15%.
ex.ko: 대량으로 사면 약 15%를 절약할 수 있다.

scandal.en: a disgraceful event or behavior that shocks people and damages someone's reputation

ex.en: The politician was involved in a corruption scandal.

scar.en: a mark left on skin after a wound has healed

ex.en: He has a scar on his arm from the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고로 팔에 흉터가 있다.

scar.en: leave a permanent mark on the body or mind

ex.en: The experience scarred her for life.
ex.ko: 그 경험은 그녀에게 평생 남을 상처를 남겼다.

ex.en: I could scarcely believe my eyes.
ex.ko: 나는 내 눈을 거의 믿을 수 없었다.

ex.en: She wore a scarlet dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 진홍색 드레스를 입고 갔다.

scent.en: a pleasant smell, especially one that is not too strong

ex.en: The scent of roses filled the garden.

scheduled.en: planned to happen at a particular time

ex.en: The flight is scheduled to depart at 9 a.m.

scheme.en: an organized plan or system for doing something

ex.en: The government introduced a new pension scheme.
He was involved in a scheme to defraud investors.

scholasticism.en: a system of philosophy and theology dominant in medieval Europe

ex.en: Scholasticism combined Christian theology with Aristotelian logic.
ex.ko: 스콜라 철학은 기독교 신학과 아리스토텔레스 논리를 결합했다.

ex.en: A group of schoolgirls walked past the shop.
ex.ko: 여학생 무리가 그 가게 앞을 지나갔다.

scientifically.en: in a way that relates to or follows the methods of science

ex.en: The claim has not been scientifically proven.

scooter.en: a small motorcycle with a low engine and small wheels

ex.en: He rides a scooter to work.

scooter.en: a child's two-wheeled vehicle pushed along by foot

ex.en: The kids rode their scooters around the park.

scramble.en: rush or struggle to do or get something before others

ex.en: Fans scrambled to get tickets for the concert.

scramble.en: cook eggs by beating and stirring them while heating

ex.en: She scrambled some eggs for breakfast.

scramble.en: a disorganized rush or struggle to get or do something

ex.en: There was a scramble for the last seats.
ex.ko: 마지막 남은 좌석을 두고 쟁탈전이 벌어졌다.

ex.en: He scribbled a note and left it on the desk.
ex.ko: 그는 급하게 메모를 휘갈겨 쓰고 책상 위에 두었다.

scribble.en: something written quickly and carelessly, or messy handwriting

ex.en: I can't read your scribble.
ex.ko: 네가 휘갈겨 쓴 글씨를 못 읽겠어.

scrooge.en: a person who is unwilling to spend or give money (informal)

ex.en: Don't be such a scrooge — buy her a nice gift.
ex.ko: 그렇게 구두쇠처럼 굴지 말고 그녀에게 좋은 선물을 사줘.

scuba.en: equipment that lets a diver breathe underwater

ex.en: They went scuba diving in the coral reef.

scythe.en: a tool with a long handle and curved blade used for cutting grass or crops

ex.en: The farmer cut the wheat with a scythe.

seal.en: a sea animal with flippers that lives partly on land

ex.en: Seals were swimming near the shore.

seal.en: close something tightly so that nothing can get in or out

ex.en: She sealed the envelope before mailing it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 편지를 부치기 전에 봉투를 봉했다.

seasonal.en: happening or existing only at a particular time of year

ex.en: Strawberries are a seasonal fruit.

second person.en: the grammatical form used to refer to the person being spoken to ("you")

ex.en: "You" is a second person pronoun.

secondary.en: less important than something else; not primary

ex.en: Money was a secondary concern compared to her health.
ex.ko: 돈은 그녀의 건강에 비하면 부차적인 문제였다.

secondly.en: used to introduce the second point in a list

ex.en: Secondly, we need to consider the cost.

sector.en: a distinct part of an economy, society, or activity

ex.en: She works in the healthcare sector.

seesaw.en: a long board balanced in the middle that two people sit on and move up and down

ex.en: The children played on the seesaw.

seesaw.en: move up and down or back and forth repeatedly

ex.en: Stock prices seesawed throughout the day.

segment.en: one of the parts that something is divided into

ex.en: The show is divided into three segments.
ex.ko: 그 프로그램은 세 부분으로 나뉘어 있다.

ex.en: The company segmented its customers by age group.
He seldom goes out on weekends.

self-confidence.en: a feeling of trust in your own abilities and judgment

ex.en: Winning the competition boosted her self-confidence.
ex.ko: 대회에서 우승한 것이 그녀의 자신감을 높여주었다.

self-confident.en: showing trust and belief in your own abilities

ex.en: He gave a self-confident speech at the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의에서 자신감 있는 연설을 했다.

selfless.en: caring more about others' needs than your own

ex.en: The nurse showed selfless dedication to her patients.
ex.ko: 그 간호사는 환자들에게 사심 없는 헌신을 보여주었다.

seller.en: a person or company that sells something

ex.en: The seller offered a discount for early payment.
ex.ko: 그 판매자는 조기 결제 시 할인을 제공했다.

semi-final.en: one of the two matches played to decide who will compete in the final

ex.en: Our team lost in the semi-final.

semicolon.en: the punctuation mark (;) used to separate parts of a sentence

ex.en: Use a semicolon to join two related sentences.
ex.ko: 관련된 두 문장을 이을 때는 세미콜론을 쓴다.

seminar.en: a meeting for discussion or training on a particular topic

ex.en: She attended a seminar on marketing strategies.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마케팅 전략에 관한 세미나에 참석했다.

ex.en: The senator voted against the bill.
ex.ko: 그 상원의원은 그 법안에 반대표를 던졌다.

sensible.en: showing good judgment; practical and reasonable

ex.en: It's sensible to save some money each month.
ex.ko: 매달 돈을 좀 저축하는 것은 현명하다.

sensitive.en: easily affected or hurt by things; quick to feel or react

ex.en: She is very sensitive to criticism.

sensitive.en: needing careful handling because it could cause problems or offense

ex.en: Salary is a sensitive topic in many workplaces.

separately.en: apart from other things or people; not together

ex.en: Please wrap the gifts separately.

serene.en: calm and peaceful, without worry or disturbance

ex.en: The lake looked serene in the morning light.
ex.ko: 그 호수는 아침 햇살 속에서 평온해 보였다.

serial.en: happening in a series, one after another, especially a story published or broadcast in parts

ex.en: She reads a serial novel published weekly.

servant.en: a person employed to do household or personal work for someone

ex.en: The wealthy family had several servants.
ex.ko: 그 부유한 가족은 하인이 여럿 있었다.

setback.en: something that delays or prevents progress

ex.en: The project suffered a major setback due to funding issues.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 자금 문제로 큰 차질을 겪었다.

sexism.en: unfair treatment of people because of their sex

ex.en: The company was criticized for sexism in hiring.

sexual.en: relating to sex or the differences between male and female

ex.en: The novel deals with sexual identity.
She wore a sexy red dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 섹시한 빨간 드레스를 입고 갔다.

shabby.en: old and in bad condition because of heavy use

ex.en: He wore a shabby old coat.
ex.ko: 그는 낡고 허름한 코트를 입고 있었다.

shabby.en: unfair or unkind in a way that shows a lack of respect

ex.en: That was a shabby way to treat a loyal employee.
ex.ko: 그것은 충성스러운 직원을 대하는 치사한 방식이었다.
